let's start off with VWOP. VWOP's a
volume weighted average price. What this
basically means is that VWAP tells us
what the average trading price of the
day is for a certain stock. This is
always a very big area. So I have a very
big rule as well. I never take calls
under VWAP and I never take puts over
VWAP. This helps me identify where
buyers and sellers are as well as VWAP
is always a big retest spot. So, if VWAP
is under the pre-market low, then I'm
looking to take puts on a VWOP retest,
not a pre-market low retest, which I'll
go over the full strategy in a bit. But,
as you can see here, how VWAP is under
our pre-market low, that means you're
taking a VWAP retest, not a pre-market
low retest. Because if it gets above
VWAP, you can't take puts. That's our
rule. And then the EMA is what I
basically use for two different reasons.
The biggest reason is it helps me
